evidence_pool.content: How All Whites' FIFA Men's World Cup venue is being transformed - Friends of Football With only four weeks to go before New Zealand’s first game at the FIFA Men’s World Cup, the Californian match venue has been undergoing a multi-billion-dollar transformation. SoFi Stadium’s transformation from an NFL venue into a FIFA World Cup stadium will be complete in time for the 2026 tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico. The $US5 billion ($NZ8.4b) venue in Inglewood, California, will host eight World Cup matches, including the United States’ opening game on June 13 (NZT), a quarter-final and, of course, the All Whites’ opening game against Iran on June 16 (NZT). The biggest challenge has been converting the stadium from an American football configuration into a FIFA-compliant football venue. SoFi Stadium was originally built with an artificial playing surface for NFL teams the Los Angeles Rams and Los Angeles Chargers. FIFA regulations require natural grass fields for World Cup matches. Before the transformation … SoFi Stadium was built for American NFL games. Construction crews have been installing a sophisticated natural grass system covering almost 6,870 square metres. The project includes a permeable ventilation and drainage layer beneath the surface, designed to help the grass survive inside the covered stadium environment. Grow lights will also be used to maintain the pitch. Officials say the playing surface will be raised about 76 centimetres above the existing turf using a specially-built substructure that allows airflow beneath the grass. Hybrid cool-season grass grown in Washington state is expected to be transported to Los Angeles in refrigerated trucks before installation. Another major change involves widening the field to FIFA’s required dimensions. An NFL field is significantly narrower than a regulation international football pitch, forcing stadium operators to remove more than 400 lower-level seats from demountable sections near the corners of the stadium. Despite the modifications, SoFi Stadium is still expected to accommodate about 70,000 spectators during the tournament. FIFA rules also mean the venue will temporarily lose its commercial naming rights and be officially known as “Los Angeles Stadium” throughout the World Cup. SoFi Stadium senior vice-president Otto Benedict told Reuters the venue’s flexible design was always intended to host global events. “It’s a limitless building,” Benedict said. The World Cup renovations are part of a remarkable run for the stadium, which will also host Super Bowl LXI in 2027 and the opening ceremony and swimming programme at the 2028 Los Angeles Olympic Games. The following video shows how the transformation was taking place: Fixtures World Cup warm-ups Game to be played on Wednesday June 3, 2026 (NZT) New Zealand v Haiti Chase Stadium, Fort Lauderdale, Florida, kick-off time 12pm (NZT) Game to be played on Sunday June 7, 2026 (NZT) New Zealand v England Raymond James Stadium, Tampa, Florida, kick-off time 8am (NZT) FIFA Men’s World Cup Game to be played on Tuesday June 16, 2026 (NZT) Iran v New Zealand SoFi Stadium, Inglewood, Los Angeles, 1pm (NZT) Game to be played on Monday June 22, 2026 (NZT) Egypt v New Zealand BC Place, Vancouver, 1pm (NZT) Game to be played on Saturday June 27, 2026 (NZT) Belgium v New Zealand BC Place, Vancouver, 3pm (NZT) The 2026 FIFA Men’s World Cup draw DOWNLOAD: Here’s the match schedule for the 2026 FIFA Men’s World Cup as a PDF >>>> READ MORE: All Whites drawn to play Belgium, Egypt and Iran at FIFA Men’s World Cup >>>> This story was first published on May 18, 2026. When you purchase through the links provided, we may earn a commission.World CupIran16 Jun 202610:00Los Angeles StadiumNew ZealandWATCH SECURELY FROM ANYWHERECeline AbrahamsJun 09, 2026 18:54+09:00Iran vs New Zealand FIFA World Cup 2026 Preview: Everything you need to knowTV Guide & StreamingIran vs New ZealandIranNew ZealandWorld CupGOAL brings you everything you need to know about Iran vs New Zealand at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. GLos Angeles StadiumIran vs New Zealand will kick-off on 16 June 2026 at 01:00 GMT and 21:00 EST (15 June).Iran vs New Zealand: Match contextThe opening match in California carries huge significance for two teams determined to make a strong start in one of the tournament’s most competitive groups. With expectations mounting at home, Iran coach Amir Ghalenoei will be looking to demonstrate that his experienced, battle-hardened squad can impose its tactical authority on football’s biggest stage, led by a clinical frontline featuring Mehdi Taremi. Standing in their way is a ambitious and rapidly developing New Zealand side led by Darren Bazeley, whose modern, possession-based approach has turned the All Whites into a technically confident outfit capable of moving the ball crisply and stretching opponents out wide. Set against the backdrop of the spectacular Los Angeles Stadium (SoFi Stadium), with its world-class, state-of-the-art facilities and iconic canopy design, the encounter promises to be one of the standout fixtures of the opening round.With formidable opponents Belgium and Egypt also competing in Group G, neither side can afford to stumble out of the blocks. Iran will view this match as the perfect opportunity to reinforce their status as one of Asia's elite powers and take a massive step toward finally breaking their historic barrier to reach the knockout rounds. New Zealand, meanwhile, arrive eager to prove that their emerging, youth-infused generation can consistently challenge the sport’s traditional heavyweights, matching the legendary resilience of their undefeated 2010 campaign. As the lights shine brightly in Southern California, the intensity and pressure of a World Cup opener will be impossible to ignore, with collective discipline, precise game management, and the ability to adapt to strict tournament rotation likely to play a decisive role in determining who claims a valuable opening victory.Read more: How to watch and live stream the 2026 FIFA World CupThe road to the 2026 FIFA World CupIran’s consistent continental commandIran stamped their ticket to North America by staging a remarkably steady, dominant run through the multi-tiered AFC qualification cycles. Competing in the highly competitive environment of the expanded Third Round group phase, Team Melli consistently imposed their experienced, high-tempo tactical authority over regional opponents to suppress any chance of qualifying drama.The primary catalyst for their successful march across the continent was an exceptional balance between defensive resilience and clinical attacking output. Under the precise guidance of Amir Ghalenoei, Iran turned their home fixtures into an unbreachable fortress while demonstrating intense collective discipline during difficult, high-pressure away trips. Spearheaded by the lethal movement and elite finishing of Mehdi Taremi up front, Iran systematically dismantled defensive blocks, mathematically booking an impressive third consecutive appearance at the global showcase with games to spare.Getty ImagesNew Zealand’s flawless Oceania expeditionWhile Iran relied on veteran composure to navigate Asia, the All Whites booked their passage to the tournament by engineering an absolute, high-octane demolition of the OFC qualification rounds. Embracing their role as clear regional heavyweights under Darren Bazeley, New Zealand left absolutely no margin for error as they systematically outpaced every challenger across Oceania.The hallmark of New Zealand's qualification journey was a progressive, possession-based approach paired with a remarkably stingy defensive record that suffocated opposing teams from the first whistle. Utilising a fluid tactical setup that stretched defenses out wide, the squad played with immense structural confidence, seamlessly blending European-based pros with breakout domestic prospects. By maintaining absolute focus, turning tricky regional away environments into comfortable victories, and securing a direct qualification spot without dropping points, New Zealand emphatically punched their ticket to the main stage for the first time since 2010.Iran vs New Zealand team newsIran team newsTeam Melli arrived at their California training base boasting an incredibly experienced, settled squad following their highly efficient march through the AFC qualification cycles. For Iran, with six World Cup appearances in their history and still chasing a first knockout-stage berth, a win here is close to mandatory if they want to control their own fate in the group. New Zealand, returning to the World Cup for only the third time after a 16-year absence, face the most winnable fixture on their schedule and know that a positive result against Iran would transform their own hopes of reaching the round of 16 for the first time.VerdictIran are the right side to back at current prices, with Mehdi Taremi carrying enough individual quality to be decisive against a New Zealand side that has limited exposure to elite attacking play. Iran to win at -118 represents a modest but defensible edge given the gap in World Cup experience and Iran’s superior forward quality.Iran vs New Zealand Match PreviewIran come into this fixture carrying the weight of history: six previous World Cup appearances, no knockout-stage finish, and an ageing squad acutely aware this could represent one of the last genuine opportunities for this generation. Manager A. Ghalenoei has built a compact, pragmatic side that relies heavily on its established veterans. The clearest attacking reference point remains Taremi, now at Olympiacos, who scored five goals across recent qualifying matches and remains the single most dangerous player on the pitch. Iran’s challenge is to translate their structural discipline into three points against a side that will cede the ball but stay organized.New Zealand, managed by M. Mayne and returning to the World Cup after a 16-year absence, are underdogs in straightforward terms but not without a tactical plan. Their 2010 campaign, in which they drew all three group matches and left the tournament unbeaten, is a reference point for a side that knows how to frustrate better-ranked opposition. This group will attempt something similar: sit deep, defend with numbers, target Chris Wood at set pieces, and take whatever transitional opportunities arrive.The contest, then, is an Iran side with attacking pedigree and defensive organisation against a New Zealand team with aerial threat, structural discipline, and the confidence of a side that dominated OFC qualifying. Where the game is won or lost is likely in the final third: whether Iran can break down a compact block, and whether New Zealand can make the most of the moments they create from direct play and dead-ball situations.Team FormIran Recent FormCosta Rica (N): Won 5-0 (Friendly, March 2026)Nigeria (N): Lost 1-2 (Friendly, March 2026)Uzbekistan (N): Drew 0-0 (Al Ain International Cup, November 2025)Cape Verde (N): Drew 0-0 (Al Ain International Cup, November 2025)Tanzania (N): Won 2-0 (Friendly, October 2025)Iran’s recent results reflect a side that has no trouble dismissing lower-ranked opposition but shows recurring signs of stagnation against organised defenses. The 5-0 win over Costa Rica confirms their attacking quality at its sharpest. However, back-to-back goalless draws in November 2025 and the loss to Nigeria point to a team that can be contained when opponents press effectively and deny space to Taremi.New Zealand Recent FormChile (H): Won 4-1 (FIFA Series, March 2026)Finland (H): Lost 0-2 (FIFA Series, March 2026)Ecuador (N): Lost 0-2 (Friendly, November 2025)Colombia (N): Lost 1-2 (Friendly, November 2025)Norway (A): Drew 1-1 (Friendly, October 2025)New Zealand’s pre-tournament form is mixed but more instructive than a surface reading suggests. The 4-1 win over Chile is their most significant recent result, evidence that against compact mid-tier opposition they can be genuinely threatening in transition. The losses to Ecuador and Colombia are more representative of what happens when they face sides that can dominate possession. That dynamic is likely to be replicated against Iran, placing the emphasis firmly on how well they manage their defensive structure.Iran vs New Zealand Head-To-HeadThese two nations have met just twice in their entire history, neither occasion at a major tournament. Iran won the most recent encounter 3-0 in a 2003 friendly. A 1973 friendly ended 0-0. evidence_pool.content: All Whites – Moss announces international retirement All Whites – Moss announces international retirement All Whites goalkeeper Glen Moss has announced his retirement from international football after more than 11 years representing New Zealand.The 35-year-old from the Newcastle Jets, who made his debut for New Zealand against Malaysia in 2006, played 29 A internationals and recorded eight clean sheets throughout an impressive career.His final tour with the team was to Peru for the away leg of the FIFA World Cup Intercontinental Playoff where the All Whites went down 2-0 to miss qualifying for Russia. Following that game he made his decision to hang up his boots. “It was not something that I took lightly. I had a good hard think about it and it is something that I have been thinking about over a period of time,” he said. “After our qualifying game over in Peru I sat down and asked myself could I go another four years? I didn’t want to start the next four year cycle and decide halfway through that I couldn’t commit to the full cycle. I decided that four years was a bit much for me at this stage of my career and with my young family as well. I have a four-year-old son and a two-year-old daughter and that played a part as well. I am comfortable with the decision. When I think back on some of the highs and lows, I will look back with fond memories.”Moss said the standout memories from his time in the All Whites were playing the big games against super-powers of world football like Brazil, Spain, Italy and Chile. “That is something that I have always relished – countries like that you can only dream of coming up against. I have the jerseys hanging on the wall at home from [Iker] Casillas from Spain and [Gianluigi] Buffon from Italy and Dida from Brazil so it is those big games that you think back on and to get to battle against those guys with some of your best mates is something I will never forget.”He has great memories of the FIFA Confederations Cup in 2009 where he played in all three games and managed a clean sheet in New Zealand’s first ever point at a senior FIFA competition in the 0-0 draw with Iraq. Moss put his body on the line in a famous shot to the face which secured the result.“I remember that face shot well,” he said with a laugh. “You kind of practice those ones in training a lot and they either hit you in the leg or the arm or the chest, but when you cop one right between the eyes like that in a FIFA tournament it doesn’t matter how you save them. That period from 2009 at the Confederations Cup and 2010 at the World Cup that was the best period for me to be part of the national side.”Paul Gothard, the All Whites goal-keeping coach, said Moss will be missed both on and off the field.“Mossy has been one of the country’s best keepers over the years and I have been lucky to work with him with the national team,” said Gothard. “He is a real professional on and off the field and it will be strange not having him around the side moving forward. I wish him all the best for the future and thank him for all he has done for the national team.”Moss will miss most the camaraderie and mate-ship of being on tour with a great group of lads.“I didn’t get to play in the 2010 World Cup but to be part of that group, it was a special team and that obviously showed through the performances to come away undefeated. The most important thing is to be part of a good group and every time we came together it was like no one had changed, whether you are Winston Reid playing in the Premiership or a Kiwi player in the local NZ competition. I always just loved being back with the boys.” Moss has decided to step away from the national team to prolong his club career and finish it off strongly. The Jets are flying in the A-League, they are currently second and right in the hunt for the minor premiership.“My body takes a bit longer to recover these days from little injuries and I couldn’t ask to be in a better club environment than to finish off my club career at the Newcastle Jets.”Moss said he leaves the All Whites in good shape with some young keepers – the likes of Michael Woud, Ollie Sail and Max Crocombe - all pushing incumbent Stefan Marinovic.“I remember when I first came on board it was Jason Batty, Michael Utting and James Bannatyne. It was me and Pasty [Mark Paston] there for four or five years and we were the only regulars in the squad. AnthonyHudson created some good depth. Stefan is the No 1 and there are a couple of young guys there who will be fighting hard for his spot. It is nice knowing that I can step away and there will be no issues in the goalkeeping department. There are two or three guys really fighting for that position for the next four years.” Glen Moss’ Career by the Numbers 29 A internationals (eight clean sheets)One FIFA World Cup (2010)Two FIFA Confederations Cups (2009 and 2017)Clean sheet in New Zealand’s first ever point at a senior FIFA competition (0-0 vs Iraq, 2009 FIFA Confederations Cup)140 …

## 共识评判

【共识】否
【理由】虽然6位智能体预测伊朗2-0胜、2位预测平局1-1，但平局方（智能体7、8）的核心论据——伍德在蹲坑反击中的威胁、伊朗进攻效率的方差、海地0-4作为友谊赛不可直接移植——未被伊朗胜方完全消解。双方在“新西兰防线脆弱性的本质”和“伊朗进攻效率的稳定性”上仍存在根本分歧，且平局方未改变预测结果，因此未达成真正共识。

---

## 最终裁定

### 三个预测（按可能性从高到低）

【预测1】胜方：伊朗 | 比分：2-0 胜 | 准确度：7/10
【预测2】胜方：伊朗 | 比分：1-0 胜 | 准确度：6/10
【预测3】胜方：平局 | 比分：1-1 | 准确度：5/10

【论点】
1. **伊朗2-0胜（最高概率）**：伊朗世界排名20 vs 新西兰85，实力差距显著。伊朗防守体系成熟（近3场零封），新西兰防线结构性脆弱（近5场连续失球，场均1.8球，且13天前对海地丢4球）。伊朗拥有塔雷米（39场28球）、莫赫比、加耶迪等多点进攻，而新西兰进攻极度依赖伍德单一支点，易被伊朗双中卫（卡纳尼、哈利勒扎德）针对性限制。模型（E031）显示伊朗胜率55.5%，是平局28.5%的近2倍。新西兰正赛0胜（3平3负）的历史数据不支持爆冷。

2. **伊朗1-0胜（次高概率）**：新西兰首战必然全力收缩防守，专注度远高于友谊赛（对英格兰仅丢1球）。伊朗面对密集防守时效率存在方差（0-0乌兹别克斯坦），可能仅靠定位球或塔雷米个人能力打入1球。但新西兰进攻产出极低（近6场3球），伍德在双人包夹下难以破门，伊朗零封概率（65-70%）高于进2球概率（50%）。

3. **平局1-1（第三概率）**：伍德状态已恢复（英超复出进球），在蹲坑反击中效率可能高于英超均值（0.34球/场）。伊朗防守零封含金量存疑（对手马里、冈比亚、哥斯达黎加均为弱旅）。首战专注度双向提升导向低比分胶着战，平局28.5%的模型概率不可忽视。但新西兰防线对海地丢4球暴露的结构性问题，使平局概率低于伊朗胜。

---

## 结构化分析

【实力对比】伊朗世界排名20，新西兰排名85，差距65位。伊朗连续4届世界杯正赛经验，新西兰世界杯正赛0胜（3平3负）。伊朗阵容身价和球员个人能力（塔雷米、贾汉巴赫什等欧洲联赛球员）显著优于新西兰。

【近期状态】伊朗近6场3胜2平1负，近3场零封（2-0马里、3-1冈比亚、5-0哥斯达黎加），防守稳固。新西兰近6场1胜5负，近5场连续失球，场均丢1.8球，13天前0-4惨败海地暴露防线结构性脆弱。

【攻防分析】伊朗进攻多点开花（塔雷米39场28球、莫赫比20场7球、加耶迪24场9球），防守体系成熟（近3场零封）。新西兰进攻极度依赖伍德（5场6球），战术单一；防线脆弱（近5场连续失球），对弱旅海地丢4球。

【场地因素】中立场（洛杉矶SoFi体育场），无主场优势。草皮为临时铺设的天然草（从华盛顿州运输），伊朗球员在亚洲区世预赛中频繁适应不同场地条件，适应性优于新西兰。此因素为边际增益，非决定变量。

【关键共识·简】所有智能体一致认为伊朗实力占优、新西兰进攻依赖伍德、伊朗防守稳固、新西兰防线存在漏洞、比赛大概率低比分（≤2球）。

【关键共识·详】
- **伊朗实力占优**：世界排名20 vs 85，阵容身价和大赛经验（连续4届世界杯）显著优于新西兰。
- **新西兰进攻依赖伍德**：所有智能体认同新西兰进攻体系极度依赖克里斯·伍德，战术单一。
- **伊朗防守稳固**：近3场零封（E005），防守体系成熟，双中卫（卡纳尼、哈利勒扎德）防空能力出色。
- **新西兰防线存在漏洞**：近5场连续失球（E010），场均丢1.8球，对海地丢4球暴露结构性问题。
- **低比分比赛**：所有智能体预测比分在1-2球区间，高比分（≥3球）概率较低。

【核心分歧·简】核心分歧在于新西兰防线脆弱性的本质（结构性崩溃 vs 友谊赛异常值）和伊朗进攻效率的稳定性（稳定2球 vs 存在方差）。

【核心分歧·详】
- **新西兰防线脆弱性的本质**：伊朗胜方（智能体1-6）认为海地0-4暴露结构性崩溃，是趋势的极端体现，正赛也会丢2球。平局方（智能体7-8）认为海地0-4是友谊赛异常值，正赛收缩防守可限制失球至1球。
- **伍德的威胁程度**：伊朗胜方认为单一支点易被双人包夹+切断传中限制。平局方认为伍德在蹲坑反击中效率可能高于英超均值（0.34球/场），且伊朗中卫未经历同类型前锋考验。
- **伊朗进攻效率的稳定性**：伊朗胜方认为面对新西兰脆弱防线可稳定打入2球。平局方指出伊朗近5场存在0-0乌兹别克斯坦的熄火场景，面对密集防守时效率有方差。
- **模型解读**：伊朗胜方认为49-55.5%胜率是明确优势信号。平局方认为两个模型差异（6.5个百分点）表明不确定性高，平局28.5%不可忽视。

【决定因素·简】新西兰防线对海地丢4球的结构性脆弱性，与伊朗多点进攻（塔雷米、莫赫比、加耶迪）的对比，是决定比赛走向的关键。

【决定因素·详】
- **新西兰防线结构性脆弱**：海地0-4是13天前的最新实战数据，不能作为异常值排除。新西兰对弱旅（海地排名约100）丢4球，对强队（英格兰）丢1球，说明其防线在面对非顶级冲击力时更容易崩溃。伊朗攻击力（塔雷米39场28球）远强于海地，即使首战专注度提升，丢2球也是合理预期。
- **伊朗进攻多点开花**：伊朗拥有塔雷米（39场28球）、莫赫比（20场7球）、加耶迪（24场9球）等多个进攻点，且定位球（卡纳尼、哈利勒扎德防空优势）是破密集防守的有效手段。新西兰防线对海地丢4球已证明其无法应对多点冲击。
- **伍德单一支点的局限性**：新西兰进攻极度依赖伍德，伊朗双中卫（卡纳尼、哈利勒扎德）防空能力出色，且可通过双人包夹+切断传中限制伍德。伍德英超效率0.34球/场，非“致命尖刀”，且刚经历重伤恢复（E044），状态存疑。
- **首战专注度的双向性**：首战专注度提升是双向的——伊朗防守纪律性更强（近3场零封），新西兰专注度提升可限制丢球数，但无法改变防线结构性问题。伊朗的防守体系（连续4届世界杯经验）在高压下更可靠。

context_analyses.travel:
## 舟车劳顿分析

- 伊朗：首轮比赛，无舟车劳顿问题，但需从亚洲长途飞往美国洛杉矶参赛，跨洲际转场可能带来时差和旅途疲劳。
- 新西兰：首轮比赛，无舟车劳顿问题，但需从大洋洲长途飞往美国洛杉矶参赛，跨洲际转场同样可能带来时差和旅途疲劳。
- 综合影响评估：两队均为首轮比赛，无前期赛程疲劳，但均需经历跨洲际长途飞行，体能储备和时差适应能力将成为潜在影响因素。
context_analyses.home_advantage:
## 主客场分析

- 名义主队伊朗与名义客队新西兰在洛杉矶SoFi Stadium进行比赛，场地为中立场地，无真正主客场之分。
- 观众支持方面，洛杉矶有较大伊朗裔社区，可能为伊朗提供更多现场支持；气候为地中海气候，两队均需适应；时差影响对两队相似，均需调整。
- 综合评估：中立场比赛，伊朗可能因社区支持获得微弱心理优势，但整体主客场因素对比赛结果影响有限。
context_analyses.pre_wc_mood:
## 赛前心态分析

- 伊朗：世界排名第20，亚洲强队，近期表现稳定，核心球员如塔雷米等状态良好，士气较高，但面对排名较低的新西兰需避免轻敌。
- 新西兰：世界排名第85，实力明显逊色，但作为世界杯新军或挑战者，心态放松，可能以拼抢和防守反击为主，压力较小。
- 综合心态对比：伊朗占据实力和心理优势，但需警惕轻敌；新西兰以弱战强，心态更轻松，可能利用对手松懈制造冷门。

structured_analysis: 伊朗世界排名20，新西兰排名85，差距65位。伊朗连续4届世界杯正赛经验，新西兰世界杯正赛0胜（3平3负）。伊朗阵容身价和球员个人能力（塔雷米、贾汉巴赫什等欧洲联赛球员）显著优于新西兰。
伊朗近6场3胜2平1负，近3场零封（2-0马里、3-1冈比亚、5-0哥斯达黎加），防守稳固。新西兰近6场1胜5负，近5场连续失球，场均丢1.8球，13天前0-4惨败海地暴露防线结构性脆弱。
伊朗进攻多点开花（塔雷米39场28球、莫赫比20场7球、加耶迪24场9球），防守体系成熟（近3场零封）。新西兰进攻极度依赖伍德（5场6球），战术单一；防线脆弱（近5场连续失球），对弱旅海地丢4球。
中立场（洛杉矶SoFi体育场），无主场优势。草皮为临时铺设的天然草（从华盛顿州运输），伊朗球员在亚洲区世预赛中频繁适应不同场地条件，适应性优于新西兰。此因素为边际增益，非决定变量。
所有智能体一致认为伊朗实力占优、新西兰进攻依赖伍德、伊朗防守稳固、新西兰防线存在漏洞、比赛大概率低比分（≤2球）。

- **伊朗实力占优**：世界排名20 vs 85，阵容身价和大赛经验（连续4届世界杯）显著优于新西兰。
- **新西兰进攻依赖伍德**：所有智能体认同新西兰进攻体系极度依赖克里斯·伍德，战术单一。
- **伊朗防守稳固**：近3场零封（E005），防守体系成熟，双中卫（卡纳尼、哈利勒扎德）防空能力出色。
- **新西兰防线存在漏洞**：近5场连续失球（E010），场均丢1.8球，对海地丢4球暴露结构性问题。
- **低比分比赛**：所有智能体预测比分在1-2球区间，高比分（≥3球）概率较低。
核心分歧在于新西兰防线脆弱性的本质（结构性崩溃 vs 友谊赛异常值）和伊朗进攻效率的稳定性（稳定2球 vs 存在方差）。

- **新西兰防线脆弱性的本质**：伊朗胜方（智能体1-6）认为海地0-4暴露结构性崩溃，是趋势的极端体现，正赛也会丢2球。平局方（智能体7-8）认为海地0-4是友谊赛异常值，正赛收缩防守可限制失球至1球。
- **伍德的威胁程度**：伊朗胜方认为单一支点易被双人包夹+切断传中限制。平局方认为伍德在蹲坑反击中效率可能高于英超均值（0.34球/场），且伊朗中卫未经历同类型前锋考验。
- **伊朗进攻效率的稳定性**：伊朗胜方认为面对新西兰脆弱防线可稳定打入2球。平局方指出伊朗近5场存在0-0乌兹别克斯坦的熄火场景，面对密集防守时效率有方差。
- **模型解读**：伊朗胜方认为49-55.5%胜率是明确优势信号。平局方认为两个模型差异（6.5个百分点）表明不确定性高，平局28.5%不可忽视。
新西兰防线对海地丢4球的结构性脆弱性，与伊朗多点进攻（塔雷米、莫赫比、加耶迪）的对比，是决定比赛走向的关键。

- **新西兰防线结构性脆弱**：海地0-4是13天前的最新实战数据，不能作为异常值排除。新西兰对弱旅（海地排名约100）丢4球，对强队（英格兰）丢1球，说明其防线在面对非顶级冲击力时更容易崩溃。伊朗攻击力（塔雷米39场28球）远强于海地，即使首战专注度提升，丢2球也是合理预期。
- **伊朗进攻多点开花**：伊朗拥有塔雷米（39场28球）、莫赫比（20场7球）、加耶迪（24场9球）等多个进攻点，且定位球（卡纳尼、哈利勒扎德防空优势）是破密集防守的有效手段。新西兰防线对海地丢4球已证明其无法应对多点冲击。
- **伍德单一支点的局限性**：新西兰进攻极度依赖伍德，伊朗双中卫（卡纳尼、哈利勒扎德）防空能力出色，且可通过双人包夹+切断传中限制伍德。伍德英超效率0.34球/场，非“致命尖刀”，且刚经历重伤恢复（E044），状态存疑。
- **首战专注度的双向性**：首战专注度提升是双向的——伊朗防守纪律性更强（近3场零封），新西兰专注度提升可限制丢球数，但无法改变防线结构性问题。伊朗的防守体系（连续4届世界杯经验）在高压下更可靠。

智能体1.stance_track: 初始→伊朗 2-0 准7 | 终局→伊朗 2-0 准7
智能体1.core_view: 伊朗防守稳固，近3场正赛零失球（E005），而新西兰场均丢1.8球且曾0-4惨败海地（E009/E010），防线结构性崩溃是主因。伍德个体威胁被过度放大，伊朗中卫防空出色。
智能体1.view_change: 立场始终未动摇，坚持用海地惨败和场均失球数据定义新西兰防线下限。
智能体1.debate_style: 强硬果断，擅长用数据直接碾压对手，面对质疑时习惯用极端案例（0-4海地）封堵所有退路。

智能体2.stance_track: 初始→伊朗 2-0 准8 | 终局→伊朗 2-0 准5
智能体2.core_view: 伊朗实力碾压，排名与攻防数据全面占优（E005）。海地案例证明新西兰防线非仅对强队脆弱，伊朗攻击力更强，2球是合理下限。
智能体2.view_change: 立场坚定，但置信度从8降至5，因在辩论中逐渐接受低比分概率上调的合理性。
智能体2.debate_style: 严谨务实，注重概率推演，善于用“对手强度调整”等逻辑拆解对方对称性论证。

智能体3.stance_track: 初始→伊朗 2-0 准7 | 终局→伊朗 2-0 准7
智能体3.core_view: 场地适应性是有效边际增益，伊朗适应新草皮经验优于新西兰（E040）。海地惨败暴露新西兰防线易崩溃，伊朗攻击力远强于海地，2球是合理下限。
智能体3.view_change: 立场未动摇，但承认“新西兰不让多进”逻辑，将2-0定位为最高概率组合而非碾压。
智能体3.debate_style: 细腻敏锐，善于挖掘被忽视的细节（草皮差异），用边际增益强化主论点，不轻易妥协。

智能体4.stance_track: 初始→伊朗 2-0 准7 | 终局→伊朗 2-0 准6
智能体4.core_view: 海地0-4是13天前最新实战数据，非异常值而是警告。攻防数据交叉验证（伊朗场均2.17球 vs 新西兰≥1.4球）指向2-0常态。
智能体4.view_change: 立场坚定，但接受低比分概率下调，拒绝3-0，最终共识大于分歧。
智能体4.debate_style: 沉稳理性，善于用交叉验证和常态预期说服人，面对分歧时主动寻求共识点。

智能体5.stance_track: 初始→伊朗 2-0 准7 | 终局→伊朗 2-0 准5
智能体5.core_view: 伍德场均0.34球非世界级尖刀（E044），伊朗防守体系曾对抗孙兴慜。海地4-0是暴露值而非异常值，防线结构性脆弱已被弱旅验证。
智能体5.view_change: 立场坚定，但置信度从7降至5，因接受定位球增量证据并建议精确化“下限”表述。
智能体5.debate_style: 冷静分析，擅长用数据趋势和对手策略差异推演进球数，对细节修正持开放态度。

智能体6.stance_track: 初始→平局 1-1 准6 | 终局→伊朗 2-0 准5
智能体6.core_view: 海地0-4是低级失误小概率爆雷，新西兰常态失球1-2球。但最终承认海地惨败暴露防线结构性崩溃，平局模型概率28.5%被历史数据修正。
智能体6.view_change: 从平局翻转至伊朗胜，关键转折点是第5次回应时承认海地案例非异常值，防线脆弱是核心败因。
智能体6.debate_style: 激情善辩，初期用双重标准攻击对手，但能根据新证据果断推翻自己立场，展现灵活性。

智能体7.stance_track: 初始→平局 1-1 准6 | 终局→平局 1-1 准5
智能体7.core_view: 伍德单点爆破能力被低估，伊朗零封含金量低（对手均弱旅）。双方专注度对称强化低比分胶着，平局与伊朗小胜共享同一场景。
智能体7.view_change: 立场摇摆但最终回归平局，经历从平局到新西兰胜再回平局的波动，坚持伍德战术价值。
智能体7.debate_style: 执着善变，反复在平局和爆冷间摇摆，但始终围绕伍德个人能力构建核心逻辑。

智能体8.stance_track: 初始→新西兰 2-1 准4 | 终局→平局 1-1 准5
智能体8.core_view: 伍德威胁被伊朗防线有效限制，新西兰进攻常态仅0.6球/场。双方数据对称性指向最平衡结果，模型49%非铁证。
智能体8.view_change: 从新西兰胜翻转至平局，关键转折是第2次回应时承认零封含金量不足，进攻效率低。
智能体8.debate_style: 理性客观，善于用对称性漏洞攻击对手，从爆冷派转为平衡派，注重模型不确定性。

group_dynamics: 火药味十足！智能体6单挑群雄，以“海地四球”为矛猛攻平局论，却遭智能体7、8用“数据对称”与“伍德破防”反杀。攻防转换如电光石火，平局与胜负的拉锯战最终僵持不下。
